docs.title: PAKE (Password Authenticated Key Exchange)
docs.text: Welcome to the documentation for the PAKE project—a clean, high-security implementation of the SPAKE2+ protocol (RFC 9383) using the Ristretto255 group and libsodium.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This project provides a robust framework for authenticating users over insecure channels without ever sending passwords or their hashes over the network. It is designed to be resistant to offline dictionary attacks and session hijacking.

Key Features

  • SPAKE2+ Protocol: A state-of-the-art balanced PAKE.
  • Ristretto255: Fast, high-security elliptic curve operations.
  • Minimal Dependencies: Uses Python's built-in ctypes to talk directly to libsodium.
  • Concurrent Server: Real-time handling of multiple authentication requests.

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
• pake/: Core protocol implementation.
  • tests/: Comprehensive test suite.
  • server.py / client.py: Networked entry points.

Get Started with Installation{ .md-button .md-button--primary }

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: To get started with the PAKE project, follow these steps to set up your environment and dependencies.

docs.title: System Dependencies
docs.text:
This project requires libsodium to be installed on your system.

sudo apt-get install libsodium-dev

docs.title: Python Dependencies
docs.text: The project is designed to be minimal. Once libsodium is installed on your system, you don't need any external Python libraries to run the core protocol, as it uses ctypes.

docs.title: Failed to load libsodium
docs.text: Try to run the command specifically with sudo. If it still fails. Close and reopen to project, or possibly your computer to refresh it. When you start the installation with sudo, also make sure to enter the right password.

docs.title: Client.py don't work
docs.text: To make sure client works, you need to run the server as well. In one terminal, run the server. In another terminal, run the client.

docs.title: How to register new user?
docs.text:
To register a new user, run the following command: 

python3 client.py register <username> <password>

docs.title: How to login?
docs.text:
To login, run the following command: 

python3 client.py login <username> <password>

docs.title: test_concurrent / test_online_auth / test_online_authM don't work
docs.text: To make sure they work, server needs to run. Try running the server in different terminal, then try to run the test file again.

docs.title: Usage Guide
docs.text: This guide covers how to run the PAKE server and interact with it using the client.

docs.title: 1. Start the Server
docs.text:
In your first terminal, start the PAKE server: 

python3 server.py

docs.title: 2. Use the Client
docs.text: In a second terminal, you can interact with the server.

docs.title: Register a new user
docs.text:
python3 client.py register <username> <password>
Example: 
python3 client.py register charlie new_password

docs.title: Login
docs.text:
python3 client.py login <username> <password>
Example: 
python3 client.py login charlie new_password

docs.title: 3. Advanced: TLS Support
docs.text: The project also includes a TLS-wrapped version of the client and server for added transport-layer security.

docs.title: Generate Certificates
docs.text: openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout key.pem -out cert.pem -days 365 -nodes

docs.title: Run TLS Server
docs.text: python3 tls_server.py

docs.title: Run TLS Client
docs.text: python3 tls_client.py

docs.title: 4. Network Simulation
docs.text:
To simulate real-world network conditions (delay/latency) on Linux: 

sudo tc qdisc add dev lo root netem delay 100ms
To remove the delay: 
sudo tc qdisc del dev lo root

docs.title: 5. Running Tests
docs.text:
To run all tests at once, simply run: 

python3 tests/
